properties.remark: SALMON IDAHO RAWS
VERY LARGE HAIL NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF US ROUTE 12 AND IDAHO HWY 11 IN GREER AROUND 2:45 PM PDT THIS AFTERNOON.
WIND GUST TO 25 MPH. .
A BUSINESS IN SALTESE REPORTED QUARTER OR BIGGER SIZED HAIL. THE HAIL DENTED CARS. THE WINDS DAMAGED SOME TREE LIMBS. STORM LASTED 15 MINUTES.
PHOTO WAS SENT TO US WITH COMPARISON TO A QUARTER. .
RECEIVED A REPORT OF 1 INCH OR LARGER HAIL FROM THE PUBLIC ON SOCIAL MEDIA.
25 FOOT POWER POLE SNAPPED. NEARBY RESIDENT REPORTED WIND WAS SO STRONG THEY COULD BARELY GET THEIR FRONT DOOR TO OPEN.
RECEIVED REPORTS OF TREES AND TREE BRANCHES DOWN IN ANACONDA FROM A STRONG WIND GUST. ALSO TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN ALONG MONTANA 1, IN THE SAME AREA. .
SUSTAINED WINDS WERE 49 MPH. MT DOT SENSOR.
HAY GOT KNOCKED DOWN AND SOME SMALL DAMAGE TO VEHICLES. CAMPER SKYLIGHTS BROKEN.
PUBLIC MEASURED 1 INCH HAIL. PHOTO SENT VERIFYING REPORT.
REPORTED AT 81 ROCK CREEK RD, CLINTON, MT 59825.
THREE VEHICLES WITH HAIL DAMAGE DENTS. BROKEN WINDSHIELD ON ONE OF THE VEHICLES. GARDEN PLANTS DEFOLIATED.
MULTIPLE PUBLIC REPORTS OF QUARTER-SIZED HAIL NEAR INTERSECTION OF HWY 93 AND 82. SMALL TWIGS AND LEAVES DOWN. VERY HEAVY RAIN.
DOWNED TREES AND MINOR FLOODING REPORTED ON MAIN STREET. .
A PERSON FROM THE PUBLIC REPORTED A SMALL METAL BUILDING WAS THROWN ACROSS A RANCH ENDING UP IN THE DITCH NEAR THE CORNER OF LONG ROAD AND US-93. .
WINDS BLEW THE ROOF OFF OF A CHICKEN HOUSE. A 15 FOOT TREE FELL OVER FOURTH OF JULY CREEK ROAD. PEA SIZE HAIL WAS ALSO REPORTED.
KRILEY CREEK RAWS REPORTED THIS PEAK WIND FROM THE SOUTHWEST OR 234 DEGREES. THIS PEAK WIND GUST COULD HAVE OCCURRED BETWEEN 1:51 AND 2:51 PM. .

DEER MOUNTAIN RAWS AT 7282 FT. PEAK WIND DIRECTION WAS SOUTHWEST OR 217 DEGREES AND OCCURRED BETWEEN 2:06 AND 3:06 PM MDT. A GUST TO 50 MPH OCCURRED SOMETIME DURING THE PREVIOUS HOUR FROM THE SOUTHWEST OR 232 DEGREES.
TEPEE POINT RAWS AT 6629 FT. PEAK WIND DIRECTION WAS FROM THE SOUTH-SOUTHWEST OR 227 DEGREES. TIME OF PEAK OCCURRED BETWEEN 2:09 AND 3:09 PM MDT.
EZRA CREEK RAWS. PEAK WIND DIRECTION WAS FROM THE SOUTHWEST, 215 DEGREES. A GUST TO 41 OCCURRED BETWEEN 1:26 AND 2:26 PM FROM THE WEST, 275 DEGREES.
KSMN AT THE LEMHI COUNTY AIRPORT. SOUTHERLY WINDS RAMPED UP TO OVER 40 MPH BY 3:20 PM. GUSTY WINDS TO 49 MPH FROM THE NORTHERLY DIRECTION OCCURRED AT 4 PM.
AN AMERICAN ELM TREE NEARLY 100 YEAR OLD IS KNOCKED DOWN. TREE WAS ABOUT 2 TO 3 FEET IN DIAMETER. .
PEAK WIND DIRECTION WAS FROM THE SOUTH OF 174 DEGREES. STATION KMTFLORE3.
REPORTS OF NUMEROUS POWER LINES AND TREE BRANCHES DOWN AROUND TOWN.
OVER 1 INCH HAIL FALLING STARTING AT 4:06PM MDT. STILL FALLING AT 4:11PM.
AFTER LIGHTNING STOPPED, SPOTTER MEASURED HAIL UP TO 1.25 INCHES IN DIAMETER. THIS SUPERSEDES THE PREVIOUS REPORT OF ESTIMATED 1 INCH HAIL FOR THIS LOCATION.
MULTIPLE RESIDENTS IN ELK BEND REPORTED NICKEL AND QUARTER-SIZED HAIL. SOME MAY HAVE BEEN LARGER ACCORDING TO A COUPLE RESIDENTS.
DAMAGE WAS REPORTED WITH THIS STORM. A FEW WINDSHIELDS WERE SMASHED IN AND A FEW CARS HAD SOME BODY DAMAGE.
LOTS OF HALF INCH TO NICKEL SIZED HAIL WITH A FEW UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS.
LOTS OF HALF INCH TO NICKEL SIZED HAIL WITH A FEW UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS.
SOME HAIL SLIGHTLY BIGGER THAN QUARTERS.
POWER OUTAGE REPORTED IN THE UPPER MILLER CREEK AREA. .
MAPLE TREE (TRUNK ESTIMATED TO BE 18 INCHES IN DIAMETER) SNAPPED IN HALF AND BLOCKED TRAFFIC FOR A SHORT TIME.
